div.h1: Cache Line Movement: False Sharing vs. Aligned Fix
div.h2: Cache coherence operates at 64-byte granularity (MESI Protocol)

div.p: Threads writing to high-frequency variables must have those variables isolated to separate cache lines (typically 64 bytes). Use alignas(64) in C++11, __attribute__((aligned(64))) in C, or @Contended in Java (JVM dependent).
Pipeline Stalls: ~400 cycles per cache miss.
Each thread writes to its own cache line. No hardware stalls.
⚠️ CRITICAL PROBLEM Both head and tail reside on the SAME 64-byte line. Any write to tail invalidates head for Core 0.

g.title: Acquires EXCLUSIVE ownership of entire 64B line
Cache MISS! Must wait for Core 0 to flush
Invalidates Core 0's line to write tail
Cache MISS! Must wait for Core 1

div.p: Core 0 Exclusive Line stays in 'M' state.

g.title: Acquires EXCLUSIVE ownership of entire 64B line
Cache MISS! Must wait for Core 0 to flush
Invalidates Core 0's line to write tail
Cache MISS! Must wait for Core 1
